problems.prompt: Insert a new book: id **9**, title `The Left Hand of Darkness`, author `Ursula K. Le Guin`, year **1969**, available **1**.
problems.hint: `INSERT INTO books (id, title, author, year, available) VALUES (…);`
problems.solution: INSERT INTO books (id, title, author, year, available) VALUES (9, 'The Left Hand of Darkness', 'Ursula K. Le Guin', 1969, 1);
problems.verify: SELECT id, title, author, year, available FROM books ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Register a new member: id **6**, name `Ada Quinn`, joined `2024-06-01`.
problems.hint: Same INSERT shape, `members` table.
problems.solution: INSERT INTO members (id, name, joined) VALUES (6, 'Ada Quinn', '2024-06-01');
problems.verify: SELECT id, name, joined FROM members ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: In a **single INSERT statement**, add two books: (9, `Kindred`, `Octavia E. Butler`, 1979, 1) and (10, `Solaris`, `Stanislaw Lem`, 1961, 1).
problems.hint: Stack two value lists: `VALUES (…), (…);`
problems.solution: INSERT INTO books (id, title, author, year, available) VALUES (9, 'Kindred', 'Octavia E. Butler', 1979, 1), (10, 'Solaris', 'Stanislaw Lem', 1961, 1);
problems.verify: SELECT id, title, author, year, available FROM books ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Insert book id **9**, title `Persuasion`, author `Jane Austen`, year **1817** — *without* mentioning the `available` column, so it takes its default.
problems.hint: List only the four columns you're providing.
problems.solution: INSERT INTO books (id, title, author, year) VALUES (9, 'Persuasion', 'Jane Austen', 1817);
problems.verify: SELECT id, title, author, year, available FROM books ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Mark book id **4** (Emma) as checked out: set `available` to 0.
problems.hint: `UPDATE books SET available = 0 WHERE id = 4;`
problems.solution: UPDATE books SET available = 0 WHERE id = 4;
problems.verify: SELECT id, available FROM books ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Member 3's name was misspelled — change it from `June Park` to `June Parks`.
problems.hint: UPDATE with a WHERE on the member id (or the old name).
problems.solution: UPDATE members SET name = 'June Parks' WHERE id = 3;
problems.verify: SELECT id, name, joined FROM members ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Append ` (Classic)` to the title of every book published **before 1900**.
problems.hint: String concatenation is `||`: `SET title = title || ' (Classic)'`.
problems.solution: UPDATE books SET title = title || ' (Classic)' WHERE year < 1900;
problems.verify: SELECT id, title FROM books ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Loan 1 was returned: set its `return_date` to `2024-06-02`.
problems.hint: UPDATE the `loans` row with id 1.
problems.solution: UPDATE loans SET return_date = '2024-06-02' WHERE id = 1;
problems.verify: SELECT id, book_id, member_id, loan_date, return_date FROM loans ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Process a full return for loan **2**: set its `return_date` to `2024-06-04` **and** mark its book (id 6) as available again. (Two statements.)
problems.hint: An UPDATE on `loans`, then an UPDATE on `books` — run both together.
problems.solution: UPDATE loans SET return_date = '2024-06-04' WHERE id = 2; UPDATE books SET available = 1 WHERE id = 6;
problems.verify: SELECT l.id, l.return_date, b.available FROM loans l JOIN books b ON b.id = l.book_id ORDER BY l.id;

problems.prompt: Library amnesty day: set **every** book's `available` to 1 — deliberately, with no WHERE clause.
problems.hint: This is the one time the missing WHERE is on purpose.
problems.solution: UPDATE books SET available = 1;
problems.verify: SELECT id, available FROM books ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Fix a records error on book **1**: set its `year` to **1966** and its `available` to **0** — in one UPDATE statement.
problems.hint: Separate the assignments with a comma: `SET year = 1966, available = 0`.
problems.solution: UPDATE books SET year = 1966, available = 0 WHERE id = 1;
problems.verify: SELECT id, title, year, available FROM books ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Delete loan record **3**.
problems.hint: `DELETE FROM loans WHERE …`
problems.solution: DELETE FROM loans WHERE id = 3;
problems.verify: SELECT id, book_id, member_id, loan_date, return_date FROM loans ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Clean up history: delete **all loans that have been returned** (their `return_date` is not NULL).
problems.hint: `WHERE return_date IS NOT NULL`.
problems.solution: DELETE FROM loans WHERE return_date IS NOT NULL;
problems.verify: SELECT id, book_id, member_id, loan_date, return_date FROM loans ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Member 5 (Lena Wolf) is moving away. Delete her **loan records first**, then her `members` row. (Two statements.)
problems.hint: Delete from `loans WHERE member_id = 5`, then from `members WHERE id = 5`.
problems.solution: DELETE FROM loans WHERE member_id = 5; DELETE FROM members WHERE id = 5;
problems.verify: SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM members WHERE id = 5) AS member_left, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM loans WHERE member_id = 5) AS loans_left;

problems.prompt: Check out a book: insert loan id **7** — book **7**, member **4**, loan date `2024-06-05`, no return date — and mark book 7 unavailable. (Two statements.)
problems.hint: INSERT into `loans` (omit `return_date` or pass NULL), then UPDATE `books`.
problems.solution: INSERT INTO loans (id, book_id, member_id, loan_date) VALUES (7, 7, 4, '2024-06-05'); UPDATE books SET available = 0 WHERE id = 7;
problems.verify: SELECT l.id, l.book_id, l.member_id, l.loan_date, l.return_date, b.available FROM loans l JOIN books b ON b.id = l.book_id ORDER BY l.id;

problems.prompt: Empty the entire `loans` table with a single statement.
problems.hint: DELETE with no WHERE — here it's intentional.
problems.solution: DELETE FROM loans;
problems.verify: SELECT COUNT(*) AS loans_left FROM loans;

problems.prompt: Bulk checkout via INSERT…SELECT: for **every available book**, insert a loan with id = book id + 100, that book's id, member **2**, date `2024-06-15` (no return date).
problems.hint: `INSERT INTO loans (id, book_id, member_id, loan_date) SELECT id + 100, id, 2, '2024-06-15' FROM books WHERE available = 1;`
problems.solution: INSERT INTO loans (id, book_id, member_id, loan_date) SELECT id + 100, id, 2, '2024-06-15' FROM books WHERE available = 1;
problems.verify: SELECT id, book_id, member_id, loan_date, return_date FROM loans ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Using a subquery, set `available = 0` for every book that has **ever been loaned** (appears anywhere in `loans`).
problems.hint: `WHERE id IN (SELECT book_id FROM loans)`.
problems.solution: UPDATE books SET available = 0 WHERE id IN (SELECT book_id FROM loans);
problems.verify: SELECT id, available FROM books ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Using a subquery, delete all loans belonging to the member named `Rosa Marin`.
problems.hint: `WHERE member_id = (SELECT id FROM members WHERE name = 'Rosa Marin')`.
problems.solution: DELETE FROM loans WHERE member_id = (SELECT id FROM members WHERE name = 'Rosa Marin');
problems.verify: SELECT id, book_id, member_id FROM loans ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: The library merged its branch records: for every member who joined **before 2024**, set `joined` to `2023-01-01`.
problems.hint: Date strings compare alphabetically, which works for ISO dates: `WHERE joined < '2024-01-01'`.
problems.solution: UPDATE members SET joined = '2023-01-01' WHERE joined < '2024-01-01';
problems.verify: SELECT id, name, joined FROM members ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Three statements: register member id **6** `Nia Bell` (joined `2024-06-18`), insert loan id **8** — book **8**, member **6**, date `2024-06-20` — and mark book 8 unavailable.
problems.hint: INSERT member → INSERT loan → UPDATE book, all in one cell.
problems.solution: INSERT INTO members (id, name, joined) VALUES (6, 'Nia Bell', '2024-06-18'); INSERT INTO loans (id, book_id, member_id, loan_date) VALUES (8, 8, 6, '2024-06-20'); UPDATE books SET available = 0 WHERE id = 8;
problems.verify: SELECT m.name, l.book_id, l.loan_date, b.available FROM loans l JOIN members m ON m.id = l.member_id JOIN books b ON b.id = l.book_id WHERE l.id = 8;

problems.prompt: Prune the catalog: delete every book that has **never been loaned**.
problems.hint: `WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT book_id FROM loans)`.
problems.solution: DELETE FROM books WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT book_id FROM loans);
problems.verify: SELECT id, title FROM books ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: A data-entry error: every loan dated `2024-05-01` actually happened on `2024-05-02`. Fix them.
problems.hint: UPDATE with a WHERE on `loan_date`.
problems.solution: UPDATE loans SET loan_date = '2024-05-02' WHERE loan_date = '2024-05-01';
problems.verify: SELECT id, loan_date FROM loans ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: One multi-row INSERT: register members **7** `Pia Chen` (joined `2024-07-01`) and **8** `Sam Ruiz` (joined `2024-07-02`).
problems.hint: Two value lists in one statement.
problems.solution: INSERT INTO members (id, name, joined) VALUES (7, 'Pia Chen', '2024-07-01'), (8, 'Sam Ruiz', '2024-07-02');
problems.verify: SELECT id, name, joined FROM members ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: Archive old records: delete every loan that started **before April 2024**.
problems.hint: `WHERE loan_date < '2024-04-01'`.
problems.solution: DELETE FROM loans WHERE loan_date < '2024-04-01';
problems.verify: SELECT id, loan_date FROM loans ORDER BY id;

problems.prompt: The finale — Omar Reyes borrows `Emma` on `2024-06-25`: insert loan id **9**, looking up the book id and member id **with subqueries** (not hard-coded numbers), then mark that book unavailable.
problems.hint: `VALUES (9, (SELECT id FROM books WHERE title = 'Emma'), (SELECT id FROM members WHERE name = 'Omar Reyes'), '2024-06-25')` — then an UPDATE with the same book subquery.
problems.solution: INSERT INTO loans (id, book_id, member_id, loan_date) VALUES (9, (SELECT id FROM books WHERE title = 'Emma'), (SELECT id FROM members WHERE name = 'Omar Reyes'), '2024-06-25'); UPDATE books SET available = 0 WHERE title = 'Emma';
problems.verify: SELECT l.id, l.book_id, l.member_id, l.loan_date, b.available FROM loans l JOIN books b ON b.id = l.book_id WHERE l.id = 9;
